title: README and CWS submission doc contradict each other on the install path
reason: The chrome-extension/README.md install section is explicitly titled "Install (local development)" and states the extension is "Not yet published to the Chrome Web Store" — so a fresh user reading it understands they're on a dev/source path. The CHROME_WEBSTORE_SUBMISSION.md is clearly an internal submission pack (checklists, $5 dev account, screenshot plan, risk register) — its "paste into Store form" text only reaches end-users after the listing goes live, at which point users install via the Web Store rather than the README. A fresh user pursuing install would land on the README, not browse into docs/ for a submission pack, so they wouldn't be torn between two paths.

title: install-autostart is macOS-only but README doesn't warn Linux/Windows users
reason: The chrome-extension/README.md never instructs users to run `install-autostart` — its Step 4 is the platform-neutral `node dist/index.js`, which works fine on Linux/Windows; the only doc mentioning `install-autostart` is the internal CWS submission doc, which already annotates the command with "auto-start server at every macOS login" inline.

title: Popup shows 'Status: loading…' with no actionable hint for unconfigured users
reason: The finding misreads the code: "loading…" only shows for the ~100ms before the first refresh() tick — after that, on a fresh install the SW returns status=null and popup.ts sets state.textContent = "unknown" (not "loading…"), and the dot is gray/unknown, not red/amber. An "Open options" button is already present and clearly visible at popup.html:25, and the README's Install step 3 explicitly tells the user to right-click → Options → paste secret before they would ever inspect the popup, so a user following the documented flow has already configured the secret. The finding overstates impact; "unknown" + a labeled "Open options" button is a reasonable cue, not a dead end.

title: Options page URL convention (`chrome-extension://<id>/options/options.html`) only works with random extension ID
reason: The `chrome-extension://<id>/options/options.html` URL appears only in `docs/CHROME_WEBSTORE_SUBMISSION.md` under the "Screenshot capture plan" — a developer-facing doc for submitting screenshots to the Chrome Web Store, not an install guide. The chrome-extension/README.md install step 3 gives the working right-click path ("Right-click the extension icon → Options → paste the secret → Save"), and never instructs the user to paste a chrome-extension://<id>/... URL. A brand-new user following the README install flow would not encounter the URL convention at all; the finding requires the user to misread an unrelated screenshot-submission doc as an install instruction, which is a contrived scenario.

title: No detection of prior install / version — silent upgrade-in-place can break the running LaunchAgent
reason: The lens is a fresh-user audit (install-vscode), and a brand-new user by definition has no prior install — no existing plist, no running LaunchAgent, no version mismatch — so all three chained commands succeed cleanly; the finding itself explicitly scopes to "Returning user re-runs setup", which is outside the fresh-user lens, and even in that off-lens case the && chain halts with a clear actionable error ("Pass --force to overwrite, or run: opscontext autostart-status") rather than a silent half-upgrade.
